files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index b99af16..0841ecd 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -167,2 +167,9 @@ Day 161 / Week 23 observations:
 - Support remains the constraint: S2 open issues are around one day old on average, and social posts still mention critical-ticket radio silence despite broad quality gains.
 - Week 23 adjustment: modestly scale S2 content, further increase critical support/reliability staffing, keep prices/capacity/dev stable, and defer tier-6 R&D until cash is above its $1M cost with buffer.
+
+Day 168 / Week 24 observations:
+- Cash rose to $1.58M, enough to fund tier-6 R&D with operating buffer. Weekly subscription payments reached $1.51M against about $902k in operating costs.
+- Scaling S2 content to $5k/day worked: 3,983 paid content subscribers from 5,935 leads, with 1,692 C and 967 B. Network effects added another 2,265 S2 subscribers.
+- Peak usage hit 6.24M/day, close to tier-4's 8M limit. Capacity should move to tier 5 before the next growth wave creates overload.
+- Cancellations rose to 317, mostly S2 A/C/B, and social posts highlight competitor quality jumps plus tax-scenario hallucinations and support timelines.
+- Week 24 adjustment: start tier-6 R&D, upgrade to capacity tier 5, hold S2 ads steady, and increase dev/support modestly to defend quality and retention.
